what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the forms/ registry; it declares that a form is a file-shape to effort-routing rule, lists the live set of forms (log, registry, decision, prose), specifies guarantees and discovery rules, and instructs how to extend and wire the registry.
observations: The registry is file-discovered: one self-registering forms/<id>.py per shape.
Each form is a module-level FORM dict with id, match callable, stage, policy, fallthrough, and desc.
The live forms are log, registry, decision, and prose (fallthrough).
route() fails loud if no form matches, so a fallthrough form is required.
Forms are deterministic recognisers (READs) and must not resolve or dispatch.
Adding a form requires both dropping a file and updating this constitution.
The registry is discovered by runtime/forms.py and consumed by the corpus capture lane (K2).
Malformed entries fail loud at discovery; non-FORM/_ files are skipped.
standouts: Effort-routing-by-form: cheap legibility pass for bookkeeping, deep pass for high-substance shapes.
File-per-entry discovery instead of a static Python dict or if-ladder.
route() must fail loudly, forcing an explicit fallthrough form.
Forms are pure READ recognisers, not dispatchers.
conventions: Uses constitution document format with YAML frontmatter and prescriptive register.
Mirrors the registry pattern used by roles/skills/projections/node-types.
Pure READ semantics: route/as_records, never resolve/dispatch.
Loud failure on malformed entries, missing matches, and acceptance-test drift.
Data-driven ordering via the fallthrough flag rather than hardcoded form names.
concerns: The fallthrough prose form matches any non-empty unit, so miscategorized low-substance content may be routed to deep.
Manual updates to AGENTS.md when adding a form duplicate the file registry and risk drift even with tests.
Match callable authoring is gated as code but its authoring path and serialization are not defined here.
Referenced generation-policy ids are not defined in this file.
notes: This is the constitution for a file-discovered shape-to-effort router that tiers capture work by form and mandates a loud-failing fallthrough.
questions: How is ordering determined among non-fallthrough forms?
What is the exact validation schema for the FORM dict?
Where are the referenced generation-policies defined?
How does runtime/forms.py discover and import the forms directory?
What is capture lane K2 and how does it consume route()?
What does create_*-authorable mean in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it prescribes the forms registry, live forms, guarantees, and extension seams without containing code.
apparent_themes: Loud failure and no silent defaults
intention_signals: It tries to institutionalize effort-routing-by-form as a data-driven, file-discovered discipline so capture depth matches corpus shape, while preventing silent mis-routing and hardcoded dispatch ladders.
novelty: It treats shape recognisers as a directory-discovered registry of self-registering files and explicitly forbids static dicts and if-ladders, while declaring that roughly half a corpus is bookkeeping deserving a cheap pass.
contribution: It contributes a prescriptive, file-discovered shape-to-effort routing vocabulary and four concrete form rules that let the capture lane tier work by corpus shape without hardcoding routing logic.
summary_for_embedding: Module constitution for a file-discovered registry of shape-to-effort routing forms that classifies corpus units as log, registry, decision, or prose and selects capture depth and generation policy accordingly.
intention_for_embedding: To make effort-routing-by-form a data-driven, file-discovered discipline that avoids wasting deep capture on bookkeeping and prevents silent unrouted units.
